response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and web software developer with strong TypeScript, Node.js, and distributed-systems credentials, but his profile has virtually no overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position requires deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, deep-learning workload analysis, and hardware-software co-design at the silicon level — none of which appear in his resume. His closest adjacent experiences are low-latency DLang programming at Dunnhumby and a bachelor's thesis on wave field synthesis with C++, but neither approaches the hardware architecture depth required for a principal-level IC in this domain.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput application development using DLang with async I/O and high-performance databases at Dunnhumby, handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Broad multi-platform engineering background spanning embedded systems, web, mobile, and desktop — demonstrating genuine technical versatility
CS degree from ETH Zürich combined with 15+ years of professional software engineering experience satisfies the baseline educational threshold
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling or silicon-level hardware architecture experience — the primary technical requirement of the role
No AI/ML or deep-learning workload expertise at any layer; the re:cinq AI work is application-level tooling (MCP frameworks), not hardware optimization
No GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or hardware-software co-design background — all listed as required or strongly preferred qualifications

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No AI accelerator, GPU, or deep-learning optimization background
Strong general software engineering depth and performance-aware programming
